engineIntelligence.h2: Chrysler Voyager Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Chrysler Voyager in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Chrysler Voyager ENR VM64C engine - diesel 2.8L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger actuator failure - causes limp mode and loss of power on higher-mileage units.
DPF blockage - exacerbated by short journeys, leading to severe emissions system issues.

cards.altText: Chrysler Voyager ENJ VM29C engine - diesel 2.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear - can send metal shavings through the fuel rails, destroying injectors.
Timing chain tensioner rattle - causes cold-start noise and potential chain stretch.

cards.altText: Chrysler Voyager EGA engine - petrol 3.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: Intake manifold gasket leaks - causes rough idle and vacuum issues on older units.
Overheating head gasket failure - often linked to aging cooling system components.

commonProblems.h2: Chrysler Voyager Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Chrysler Voyager’s reliability is heavily tied to its engine choice. The 2.5L and 2.8L CRD diesels dominate UK replacements, often failing due to high-pressure fuel pump wear or turbo/DPF issues. The 3.3L V6 is generally robust but prone to cooling-related head gasket failures. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address these original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: Chrysler Voyager 2.5 CRD High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure
problems.h4: Chrysler Voyager 2.5 CRD High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - Why Injectors Get Destroyed
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Voyager 2.5 CRD SE, 2.5 CRD LX (2001-2008, ENJ & VM29C)
problems.rootCause: The internal components of the VM Motori high-pressure fuel pump degrade, shedding microscopic metal shavings into the fuel rails.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing the HPFP, flushing the entire fuel system, and replacing all injectors.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before metal shavings cause catastrophic internal engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Installing a complete engine with a brand-new, upgraded high-pressure fuel pump and fresh injectors.
repairOptions.longevity: The definitive fix if metal contamination has already scored cylinder walls or damaged bearings.

problems.recommendation: On a Voyager with over 100,000 miles, a failing HPFP is a major red flag. If the engine is already running rough or making knocking sounds, a partial repair is a false economy. Opting for a full rebuilt engine ensures the entire fuel and internal system is fresh and reliable.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt ENJ/VM29C engine prices for your Chrysler Voyager

problems.group: Chrysler Voyager 2.8 CRD Turbo & DPF Failure
problems.h4: Chrysler Voyager 2.8 CRD Turbo & DPF Failure - Why Limp Mode Occurs
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Voyager 2.8 CRD Executive, LX, SE (2005-2008, ENR & VM64C)
problems.rootCause: Worn piston rings cause excessive blow-by, overwhelming the DPF with soot and oil, while the turbo actuator seizes from heat and carbon buildup.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing or reconditioning the turbo, and cleaning or replacing the DPF and EGR valve.
repairOptions.longevity: A temporary fix if the underlying engine wear (blow-by) is not addressed first.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Installing a reconditioned engine with updated rings, a new turbo, and a clean DPF system.
repairOptions.longevity: The only permanent solution to resolve chronic DPF blockages caused by internal engine wear.

problems.recommendation: Simply replacing the DPF or turbo without addressing the root cause (blow-by from worn internals) will result in the new components failing again. A reconditioned engine resolves the root cause, protecting your new emissions components.
problems.cta: Get quotes for ENR/VM64C engine replacement from UK specialists

problems.group: Chrysler Voyager 3.3 V6 Overheating & Head Gasket Failure
problems.h4: Chrysler Voyager 3.3 V6 Overheating & Head Gasket Failure
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Voyager 3.3 V6 LE, LX, SE (2001-2008, EGA)
problems.rootCause: Aging plastic cooling system components (thermostat housing, water pump) fail, leading to rapid overheating and warped cylinder heads.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing the water pump, thermostat, hoses, and pressure testing the system.
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance. Only viable if the head gasket is not yet blown.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Installing a reconditioned V6 with a fully refreshed, upgraded cooling system and new head gaskets.
repairOptions.longevity: The safest route if the engine has already overheated and suffered internal thermal damage.

problems.recommendation: The EGA V6 is a robust engine, but it is highly sensitive to overheating. If you suspect a head gasket failure (milky oil or white smoke), a full engine replacement is often more cost-effective than a complex machine shop rebuild of a warped block.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EGA V6 engine prices for your Chrysler Voyager

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Chrysler Voyager
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're battling HPFP issues in your 2.5 CRD, turbo failures in your 2.8 CRD, or cooling woes in your 3.3 V6, EngineMarket makes it simple to find a lasting solution. Our network of trusted UK Chrysler Voyager specialists provides rebuilt and reconditioned engines with upgraded components to fix the original design flaws, all backed by a 12-month warranty and available with nationwide supply & fit. Compare prices today and get back on the road without paying the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Chrysler Voyager Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Chrysler Voyager and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Chrysler Voyager Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Chrysler Voyager's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The all-new fourth-generation Voyager launched with a modernised design and a choice of robust, proven Chrysler and VM Motori powertrains.
keyChanges: Introduction of the new RS-platform Chrysler Voyager
Launch of the 2.4L EDZ petrol and 3.3L EGA V6 petrol engines
Introduction of the 2.5L CRD (ENJ/VM29C) diesel for the UK market
mainEngines: EGA - 3.3L petrol (3.3 V6 SE, 3.3 V6 LX, 3.3 V6 LE)
ENJ/VM29C - 2.5L diesel (2.5 CRD SE, 2.5 CRD LX)
knownFor: Spacious, theatre-style seating and innovative storage solutions
The 3.3L V6 is widely considered the most reliable engine of this era
Early 2.5 CRD models require vigilant HPFP monitoring
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2004 Chrysler Voyager replacement engines
years.ticker: Chrysler Voyager 2001 engine replacement • Chrysler Voyager 3.3 V6 engine swap • 2.5 CRD HPFP failure fix

years.description: A significant mid-life update brought refined styling, improved interiors, and the highly desirable 2.8L CRD diesel to the UK lineup.
keyChanges: Exterior and interior facelift for a more modern appearance
Introduction of the 2.8L CRD (ENR/VM64C) diesel, replacing the 2.5L in higher trims
Launch of the flagship 3.8L V6 (EGH) in the new Limited trim
mainEngines: EGA - 3.3L petrol (3.3 V6 SE, 3.3 V6 LX, 3.3 V6 LE)
ENJ/VM29C - 2.5L diesel (2.5 CRD SE, 2.5 CRD LX)
ENR/VM64C - 2.8L diesel (2.8 CRD SE, 2.8 CRD LX, 2.8 CRD Executive)
knownFor: The 2.8 CRD Executive became the definitive UK family people carrier
Improved refinement and reduced cabin noise across all variants
DPF and turbo issues become the primary failure points for the 2.8L
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2008 Chrysler Voyager replacement engines

faq.h2: Chrysler Voyager Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Chrysler Voyager engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A complete Chrysler Voyager engine replacement typically costs between £1,200 and £3,500 for a rebuilt or reconditioned unit, supply only. Adding supply and fit will increase this by around £500-£1,200.
keyPoints: 3.3L V6 petrol engines are the most affordable, starting from £1,200.
2.5L CRD diesel engines typically range from £1,500 to £2,800.
2.8L CRD diesel engines cost more, usually between £1,800 and £3,200.
Prices vary based on the specific engine code and supplier.
items.cta: Compare Chrysler Voyager engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What is the most reliable replacement engine for a Chrysler Voyager?
items.answer: The most reliable replacement is a professionally rebuilt 3.3L V6 (EGA) due to its simple overhead valve design, or a rebuilt 2.8L CRD with updated turbo and DPF components.
keyPoints: A rebuilt engine corrects known failure points with new parts.
The 3.3L V6 is less complex and highly durable when cooled properly.
Avoid cheap used engines for high-mileage diesel vehicles.
Always choose a unit with a minimum 12-month warranty.
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Chrysler Voyager engine with 12-month warranty

items.question: Can I get a reconditioned Chrysler Voyager engine with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all reconditioned and rebuilt Chrysler Voyager engines supplied through our network come with a minimum 12-month, unlimited-mileage warranty as standard.
keyPoints: The warranty covers all internal mechanical parts and gaskets.
Labour for warranty claims is often included via supply & fit.
This provides crucial peace of mind compared to unverified used engines.
Always confirm the exact terms with your chosen supplier.
items.cta: Get quotes for Chrysler Voyager engine replacement from UK specialists

items.question: How do I find the correct engine code for my Chrysler Voyager?
items.answer: Your engine code can be found on your V5C logbook, by checking the 8th digit of your VIN, or stamped on the engine block near the oil filter housing.
keyPoints: The 8th VIN digit is the quickest way to identify your engine family.
Your V5C logbook will list the engine code under 'engine number'.
The physical stamp on the block is the most definitive method.
Providing the exact code ensures you get the correct replacement.
items.cta: Enter your reg to instantly find your Chrysler Voyager engine code

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Chrysler Voyager?
items.answer: Yes, if the rest of the car (bodywork, transmission, interior) is in good condition, a £1,500-£3,000 engine replacement is far more economical than scrapping the car.
keyPoints: The Chrysler Voyager is a robust vehicle with a strong used market.
Main dealers often quote prices that exceed the car's worth.
A specialist-supplied engine restores reliability for a fraction of the cost.
It extends the life of a vehicle you are already familiar with.
items.cta: Get a free quote to see if engine replacement is right for you

items.question: What causes the 2.5 CRD high-pressure fuel pump to fail?
items.answer: Internal component degradation in the VM Motori HPFP sheds microscopic metal shavings into the fuel rails, destroying the injectors and potentially the engine.
keyPoints: It is a well-documented weakness of the 2.5 CRD engine.
Symptoms include rough idling, loss of power, and metallic tapping.
A full fuel system flush is mandatory if the pump fails.
Rebuilt engines include a brand-new, upgraded fuel pump.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt ENJ/VM29C engine prices for your Chrysler Voyager

items.question: Do you offer a supply and fit service for Chrysler Voyager engines?
items.answer: Yes, absolutely. EngineMarket connects you with a network of RAC-approved UK garages who offer a complete supply and fit service with a single, consolidated warranty.
keyPoints: We handle sourcing the engine and booking the garage.
The engine is delivered directly to your local approved garage.
You get one invoice and one warranty covering parts and labour.
This removes the hassle of finding a willing fitting garage.
items.cta: Book your Chrysler Voyager engine supply and fit today

items.question: How long does a Chrysler Voyager engine replacement take?
items.answer: A professional supply and fit of a Chrysler Voyager engine typically takes 1 to 2 days once the engine has arrived at the garage, with total turnaround times of 3 to 5 working days.
keyPoints: We coordinate next-day delivery of the engine to your local garage.
The Voyager's engine bay is relatively accessible for specialists.
You receive a single invoice covering both the engine and labour.
This eliminates the risk of a garage refusing a customer-supplied part.
items.cta: Check live Chrysler Voyager engine supply and fit turnaround times

items.question: Will a 2005 Chrysler Voyager engine fit a 2003 model?
items.answer: Not always. While the 3.3L V6 block is similar, the 2005+ facelift models feature updated wiring, sensors, and ECU software that may not be compatible with 2001-2004 cars.
keyPoints: Official cross-compatibility is not guaranteed across generations.
ECU remapping and custom wiring may be required for swaps.
We strongly recommend direct, OEM-code replacements for reliability.
Our system instantly matches your reg to the precise compatible engine.
items.cta: Enter your reg to find the correct engine for your Chrysler Voyager year

items.question: Can you source a used Chrysler Voyager engine with low mileage?
items.answer: Yes, we can source verified low-mileage used Chrysler Voyager engines from our network, all of which undergo basic compression and leak-down testing before dispatch.
keyPoints: We connect you with over 100 UK engine specialists and breakers.
Mileage verification and history checks are prioritised for all units.
You can compare price, mileage, and warranty side-by-side online.
Delivery to your chosen garage is arranged seamlessly by our team.
